expectations.text: Phase 0 context loading is performed or acknowledged before investigation begins
expectations.evidence: No Phase 0 or context loading step is present. The transcript jumps directly into 'Investigation' analyzing the error and code without any structured context loading phase.

expectations.text: Phase 1 dispatches at least 2 parallel investigation subagents (Error Analysis and Change Analysis at minimum)
expectations.evidence: No subagents are dispatched. The transcript is a single-threaded analysis without any mention of subagents, parallel investigation, or dispatching.

expectations.text: Orchestrator provides a status update between dispatches including current phase and what is being investigated
expectations.evidence: No orchestrator, no status updates, no phase tracking. The transcript follows a simple linear structure: Investigation -> Root Cause -> Fix -> Additional Recommendations.

expectations.text: A synthesis report is produced consolidating Phase 1 findings before hypothesis formation
expectations.evidence: No synthesis report exists. The transcript moves directly from investigation to 'Root Cause' without any consolidation step.

expectations.text: Phase 3 hypothesis specifically identifies string interpolation in SQL as the root cause — not just 'SQL error' or 'special characters'
expectations.evidence: Root Cause section states: 'String interpolation is used to embed user-provided values directly into SQL queries without any parameterization or escaping.' This is specific and accurate despite not being labeled as a 'Phase 3 hypothesis'.

expectations.text: Phase 3.5 red-teams the hypothesis before implementation proceeds
expectations.evidence: No red-teaming step exists. The transcript goes directly from Root Cause to Fix without any adversarial challenge of the hypothesis.

expectations.text: Phase 4 implementer writes a failing test BEFORE implementing the fix
expectations.evidence: No tests are written at all. The transcript provides the fix code directly without any test-first approach or TDD methodology.

expectations.text: The fix uses parameterized queries or prepared statements — not string escaping as a band-aid
expectations.evidence: Fix uses parameterized queries: "const query = 'INSERT INTO feedback (name, email, comment) VALUES (?, ?, ?)'" and the 'Why Not String Escaping?' section explicitly argues against mysql.escape() or manual escaping.

expectations.text: Phase 5 quality gate (red-team and/or code review) is performed on the fix
expectations.evidence: No quality gate, red-team review, or code review is performed on the fix. The transcript ends with 'Additional Recommendations' and 'Why Not String Escaping?' but these are not structured reviews of the fix quality.

expectations.text: Orchestrator narrates each phase transition with hypothesis status and cycle count
expectations.evidence: No orchestrator narration, no phase transitions, no hypothesis status tracking, no cycle counts. The transcript has no structured phase system.

expectations.text: The fix addresses the root cause (SQL injection vulnerability) not just the symptom (500 error on special characters)
expectations.evidence: The transcript explicitly identifies SQL injection: 'This is a SQL injection vulnerability, not just an encoding bug.' The fix section states parameterized queries 'eliminates both the syntax error and the SQL injection vulnerability.' The 'Why Not String Escaping?' section reinforces this.

claims.claim: The root cause was correctly identified as string interpolation / SQL injection
claims.evidence: Root Cause section clearly identifies string interpolation and SQL injection.

claims.claim: The fix is technically correct (parameterized queries)
claims.evidence: Uses '?' placeholders with the mysql driver's parameterized query support.

claims.claim: The without_skill approach lacks structured debugging methodology
claims.evidence: No phases, no subagents, no synthesis, no red-teaming, no TDD, no quality gate. This is a straightforward single-pass analysis.

suggestions.assertion: Phase 0 context loading is performed or acknowledged before investigation begins
suggestions.reason: This assertion inherently tests for the skill's phased methodology. Without the skill, there is no Phase 0. The assertion is fair for comparing with_skill vs without_skill but will always fail for without_skill runs by design.

suggestions.assertion: Phase 1 dispatches at least 2 parallel investigation subagents
suggestions.reason: Same issue -- this is a methodology-specific assertion that tests skill compliance rather than debugging quality. The without_skill run correctly identified the root cause and fix without subagents.

eval_feedback.overall: The expectations are heavily weighted toward process compliance (8 of 11 are process/methodology assertions). The without_skill run passes all 3 domain-correctness assertions (correct root cause, correct fix, addresses security) but fails all 8 process assertions because it has no structured methodology. This is by design for eval comparison, but it means the without_skill pass rate underrepresents the quality of the debugging outcome. Consider adding a separate domain-accuracy score alongside the process-compliance score to provide a more nuanced comparison. The without_skill transcript is concise, accurate, and includes useful extras (error handling recommendation, OWASP reference, audit suggestion) that the eval doesn't measure.
